In [49]:
import duckdb
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

BASE = '../../../versioned'

con = duckdb.connect()
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW done_urls AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_done_urls/article_done_urls.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW scores AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_koryciarski_scores/article_koryciarski_scores.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW facts AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_facts/article_facts_qwen32.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW analyzed AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_analyzed/article_analyzed_qwen32.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW analyzed_qwen32 AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_analyzed/article_analyzed_qwen32.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW facts_qwen32 AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_facts/article_facts_qwen32.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW parsed AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_parsed/article_parsed.jsonl', ignore_errors=true)")
print('Views ready')

Views ready

Total Articles by Source¶

In [50]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        source,
        count(*) AS urls,
        count(DISTINCT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1)) AS domains
    FROM done_urls
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 2 DESC
""").df()
Out[50]:
source urls domains
0 postgres 1303556 1842
1 url_store 131526 526

Source Intersection — URL overlap¶

In [51]:
con.execute("""
    WITH pg AS (
        SELECT regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', '') AS url FROM done_urls WHERE source = 'postgres'
    ),
    us AS (
        SELECT regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', '') AS url FROM done_urls WHERE source = 'url_store'
    )
    SELECT
        'only postgres'  AS bucket, count(*) AS links FROM pg WHERE url NOT IN (SELECT url FROM us)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'only url_store', count(*) FROM us WHERE url NOT IN (SELECT url FROM pg)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'in both',        count(*) FROM pg WHERE url IN (SELECT url FROM us)
""").df()
Out[51]:
bucket links
0 only postgres 1300700
1 only url_store 128670
2 in both 2856

Naszemiasto: postgres vs url_store coverage¶

In [52]:
con.execute("""
    WITH pg_domains AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain
        FROM done_urls WHERE source = 'postgres' AND split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) LIKE '%naszemiasto%'
    ),
    us_domains AS (
        SELECT DISTINCT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain
        FROM done_urls WHERE source = 'url_store' AND split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) LIKE '%naszemiasto%'
    )
    SELECT 'only postgres' AS bucket, domain FROM pg_domains WHERE domain NOT IN (SELECT domain FROM us_domains)
    UNION ALL
    SELECT 'only url_store', domain FROM us_domains WHERE domain NOT IN (SELECT domain FROM pg_domains)
    ORDER BY 1, 2
""").df()
Out[52]:
bucket domain
0 only postgres kodyrabatowe.naszemiasto.pl
1 only postgres malopolskie.naszemiasto.pl
2 only postgres naszemiasto.pl
3 only url_store andrychow.naszemiasto.pl
4 only url_store bielawa.naszemiasto.pl
... ... ...
104 only url_store ziebice.naszemiasto.pl
105 only url_store zielonka.naszemiasto.pl
106 only url_store zmigrod.naszemiasto.pl
107 only url_store zukowo.naszemiasto.pl
108 only url_store zychlin.naszemiasto.pl

109 rows × 2 columns

Urls per Domain (top 40)¶

In [53]:
df_domains = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain,
        count(*) AS urls,
        sum(CASE WHEN source = 'url_store' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS from_url_store,
        sum(CASE WHEN source = 'postgres' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS from_postgres
    FROM done_urls
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 2 DESC
    LIMIT 40
""").df()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 8))
d = df_domains[::-1]
ax.barh(d['domain'], d['from_postgres'], label='postgres', color='steelblue')
ax.barh(d['domain'], d['from_url_store'], left=d['from_postgres'], label='url_store', color='tomato')
ax.set_xlabel('Articles')
ax.set_title('Top 40 domains by url count (blue=postgres, red=url_store)')
ax.legend()
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
df_domains
Out[53]:
domain urls from_url_store from_postgres

Urls per Domain — All Domains (rank plot)¶

In [55]:
df_all = con.execute("""
    SELECT count(*) AS urls
    FROM done_urls
    GROUP BY split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1)
    ORDER BY 1 DESC
""").df()

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(14, 5))
ax.plot(range(1, len(df_all) + 1), df_all['urls'])
ax.set_xlabel('Domain rank')
ax.set_ylabel('Urls')
ax.set_title(f'Urls per domain — all {len(df_all)} domains (log scale)')
ax.set_yscale('log')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
print(f'Total domains: {len(df_all)}')
print(f'Median urls/domain: {df_all["urls"].median():.0f}')
print(f'Top domain: {df_all["urls"].iloc[0]:,} articles')

Total domains: 1948
Median urls/domain: 228
Top domain: 44,782 articles

Parsing: Status Breakdown¶

In [64]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        'total_crawled_urls' AS stage,
        count(*) AS count,
        count(DISTINCT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1)) AS domains
    FROM done_urls
    UNION ALL
    SELECT
        parse_status AS stage,
        count(*) AS count,
        count(DISTINCT split_part(url, '/', 1)) AS domains
    FROM parsed
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY count DESC
""").df()
Out[64]:
stage count domains
0 total_crawled_urls 1435082 1948
1 selector_not_found 726605 1302
2 ok 503602 1389
3 empty_text 49352 114
4 error 1601 3

Domains with ≥50% Pages Parsed OK¶

In [65]:
half_ok = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        split_part(url, '/', 1) AS domain,
        count(*) AS total_pages,
        sum(CASE WHEN parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ok_pages,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS ok_pct
    FROM parsed
    GROUP BY 1
    HAVING ok_pct >= 50
""").df()

print(f'Domains with ≥50% ok: {len(half_ok)}')
print()
print('--- 10 random ---')
display(half_ok.sample(10))
print('--- 10 biggest ---')
display(half_ok.nlargest(10, 'total_pages'))
print('--- 10 smallest ---')
display(half_ok.nsmallest(10, 'total_pages'))

Domains with ≥50% ok: 921

Parsing: Extraction Method¶

In [59]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        extraction_method,
        count(*) AS pages,
        round(100.0 * count(*) / sum(count(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
    FROM parsed
    WHERE parse_status = 'ok'
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 2 DESC
""").df()
Out[59]:
extraction_method pages pct
0 selector 487247 96.8
1 NaN 16355 3.2

Parsed Pages: Metadata Coverage¶

In [68]:
# recreate view with ignore_errors in case setup cell was not rerun
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW parsed AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_parsed/article_parsed.jsonl', ignore_errors=true)")

con.execute("""
    SELECT
        count(*) AS total_parsed,
        sum(CASE WHEN parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ok,
        sum(CASE WHEN title IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_title,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN title IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS title_pct,
        sum(CASE WHEN publication_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_date,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN publication_date IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS date_pct,
        sum(CASE WHEN ld_json IS NOT NULL AND ld_json::VARCHAR != 'null' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_ld_json,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN ld_json IS NOT NULL AND ld_json::VARCHAR != 'null' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS ld_json_pct
    FROM parsed
""").df()
Out[68]:
total_parsed ok has_title title_pct has_date date_pct has_ld_json ld_json_pct
0 1281160 503602.0 12852.0 1.0 12852.0 1.0 1157411.0 90.3

Koryciarski Scores: Distribution among OK Parsed Pages¶

In [70]:
# Score scale:
# 0 = no connection to koryciarstwo
# 1 = mentions politicians/officials, no abuse context
# 2 = describes appointments/promotions/decisions without explicit criticism
# 3 = suggests nepotism, conflicts of interest or position-filling
# 4 = directly describes corruption/nepotism/appointing by connections
# 5 = main topic is koryciarstwo, documented specific cases
# not_article = page is not an article (listing, template, menu etc.)
# llm_error = API/parse error during scoring

con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW scores AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_koryciarski_scores/article_koryciarski_scores.jsonl')")

con.execute("""
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN error IS NOT NULL THEN 'llm_error'
            WHEN llm_is_article = false THEN 'not_article'
            ELSE koryciarski_llm_score::VARCHAR
        END AS score,
        count(*) AS pages,
        round(100.0 * count(*) / sum(count(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
    FROM scores
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY
        CASE
            WHEN score = 'llm_error' THEN 98
            WHEN score = 'not_article' THEN 99
            ELSE score::INT
        END
""").df()
Out[70]:
score pages pct
0 0 333009 66.1
1 1 54947 10.9
2 2 9763 1.9
3 3 22571 4.5
4 4 2683 0.5
5 5 677 0.1
6 llm_error 629 0.1
7 not_article 79323 15.8

Domains with ≥50% Pages Scored as Articles (score 0-5), min 10 URLs¶

In [72]:
# join done_urls to get domain, then check what fraction of each domain's URLs got a score
scored_domains = con.execute("""
    WITH domain_counts AS (
        SELECT
            split_part(regexp_replace(d.url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain,
            count(*) AS total_urls,
            sum(CASE WHEN s.koryciarski_llm_score IS NOT NULL AND s.error IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS scored,
            round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN s.koryciarski_llm_score IS NOT NULL AND s.error IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS scored_pct
        FROM done_urls d
        LEFT JOIN scores s ON regexp_replace(d.url, '^https?://', '') = s.url
        GROUP BY 1
    )
    SELECT * FROM domain_counts WHERE scored_pct >= 50 AND total_urls >= 10
    ORDER BY total_urls DESC
""").df()

print(f'Domains with ≥50% scored: {len(scored_domains)}')
print()
print('--- 10 random ---')
display(scored_domains.sample(min(10, len(scored_domains))))
print('--- 10 biggest ---')
display(scored_domains.nlargest(10, 'total_urls'))
print('--- 10 smallest ---')
display(scored_domains.nsmallest(10, 'total_urls'))

Domains with ≥50% scored: 657

Parsed & Scored Rate: postgres vs url_store¶

In [74]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        d.source,
        count(*) AS total_urls,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ok,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN p.parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS ok_pct,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.parse_status = 'selector_not_found' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS selector_not_found,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN p.parse_status = 'selector_not_found' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS selector_not_found_pct,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.parse_status = 'empty_text' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS empty_text,
        sum(CASE WHEN p.url IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS not_parsed,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN p.url IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS not_parsed_pct,
        sum(CASE WHEN s.koryciarski_llm_score IS NOT NULL AND s.error IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS scored,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN s.koryciarski_llm_score IS NOT NULL AND s.error IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS scored_pct
    FROM done_urls d
    LEFT JOIN parsed p ON regexp_replace(d.url, '^https?://', '') = p.url
    LEFT JOIN scores s ON regexp_replace(d.url, '^https?://', '') = s.url
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 1
""").df()
Out[74]:
source total_urls ok ok_pct selector_not_found selector_not_found_pct empty_text not_parsed not_parsed_pct scored scored_pct
0 postgres 1303556 503602.0 38.6 726605.0 55.7 49352.0 22396.0 1.7 423650.0 32.5
1 url_store 131526 1740.0 1.3 1115.0 0.8 1.0 128670.0 97.8 1710.0 1.3

Domain Score Metrics (min 10 scored pages)¶

In [75]:
domain_metrics = con.execute("""
    SELECT
        split_part(url, '/', 1) AS domain,
        count(*) AS scored_pages,
        round(avg(koryciarski_llm_score), 3) AS mean_score,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN koryciarski_llm_score >= 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 2) AS pct_score4plus,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN koryciarski_llm_score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 2) AS pct_score5
    FROM scores
    WHERE koryciarski_llm_score IS NOT NULL AND error IS NULL AND llm_is_article = true
    GROUP BY 1
    HAVING count(*) >= 10
""").df()

top5_domains = domain_metrics.nlargest(5, 'scored_pages')['domain'].tolist()

for metric, label in [('mean_score', 'Mean Score'), ('pct_score4plus', '% Score 4+'), ('pct_score5', '% Score 5')]:
    print(f'\n=== {label} ===')
    print('Top 5:')
    display(domain_metrics.nlargest(5, metric)[['domain', 'scored_pages', metric]])
    print('Bottom 5:')
    display(domain_metrics.nsmallest(5, metric)[['domain', 'scored_pages', metric]])

print('\n=== 5 Biggest Domains ===')
display(domain_metrics[domain_metrics['domain'].isin(top5_domains)][['domain', 'scored_pages', 'mean_score', 'pct_score4plus', 'pct_score5']])

=== Mean Score ===

Facts: Type Distribution¶

In [81]:
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW facts AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_facts/article_facts_qwen32.jsonl')")
con.execute(f"CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW analyzed AS SELECT * FROM read_json_auto('{BASE}/article_analyzed/article_analyzed_qwen32.jsonl')")

con.execute("""
    SELECT
        CASE
            WHEN fact->>'fact_type' IS NOT NULL THEN fact->>'fact_type'
            WHEN fact->>'party' IS NOT NULL THEN 'party_membership'
            WHEN fact->>'subject' IS NOT NULL THEN 'personal_relation'
            WHEN fact->>'person' IS NOT NULL THEN 'employment'
            ELSE 'unknown'
        END AS fact_type,
        count(*) AS total,
        round(100.0 * count(*) / sum(count(*)) OVER (), 1) AS pct
    FROM facts,
    LATERAL unnest(extracted_facts) AS t(fact)
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 2 DESC
""").df()
Out[81]:
fact_type total pct
0 employment 35090 67.4
1 party_membership 13977 26.9
2 personal_relation 2960 5.7

Facts: Justification Match Rate Overall¶

In [82]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        count(*) AS total_facts,
        sum(CASE WHEN fact->>'justification_in_text' IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matched,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN fact->>'justification_in_text' IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS match_pct
    FROM facts,
    LATERAL unnest(extracted_facts) AS t(fact)
""").df()
Out[82]:
total_facts matched match_pct
0 52027 44460.0 85.5

Facts: Justification Match Rate by Koryciarski Score¶

In [83]:
con.execute("""
    SELECT
        a.koryciarski_llm_score AS score,
        count(*) AS total_facts,
        sum(CASE WHEN fact->>'justification_in_text' IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS matched,
        round(100.0 * sum(CASE WHEN fact->>'justification_in_text' IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / count(*), 1) AS match_pct
    FROM analyzed a,
    LATERAL unnest(a.extracted_facts) AS t(fact)
    GROUP BY 1
    ORDER BY 1 NULLS LAST
""").df()
Out[83]:
score total_facts matched match_pct
0 2 30229 25594.0 84.7
1 3 19583 16927.0 86.4
2 4 1727 1528.0 88.5
3 5 488 411.0 84.2

Facts: Sample Articles — Top 3 Domains (2 each)¶

In [87]:
import json
top3 = con.execute("""
    SELECT domain, count(*) AS cnt
    FROM analyzed
    GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 3
""").df()['domain'].tolist()

for domain in top3:
    print(f'\n=== {domain} ===')
    rows = con.execute("""
        SELECT url, koryciarski_llm_score, extracted_facts
        FROM analyzed WHERE domain = ? LIMIT 2
    """, [domain]).df()
    for _, r in rows.iterrows():
        print(f'  https://{r["url"]}')
        print(f'  score: {r["koryciarski_llm_score"]}')
        for f in r['extracted_facts']:
            print(f'    {json.dumps({k: v for k, v in f.items() if k != "url"}, ensure_ascii=False)}')
        print()

Facts: Sample Articles — 3 Random Domains (2 each)¶

In [88]:
import random
all_domains = con.execute("SELECT DISTINCT domain FROM analyzed").df()['domain'].tolist()
random_domains = random.sample(all_domains, 3)

for domain in random_domains:
    print(f'\n=== {domain} ===')
    rows = con.execute("""
        SELECT url, koryciarski_llm_score, extracted_facts
        FROM analyzed WHERE domain = ? LIMIT 2
    """, [domain]).df()
    for _, r in rows.iterrows():
        print(f'  https://{r["url"]}')
        print(f'  score: {r["koryciarski_llm_score"]}')
        for f in r['extracted_facts']:
            print(f'    {json.dumps({k: v for k, v in f.items() if k != "url"}, ensure_ascii=False)}')
        print()

Facts: Sample Articles — 1 per Koryciarski Score¶

In [92]:
for score in [2, 3, 4, 5]:
    print(f'\n=== Score {score} ===')
    rows = con.execute("""
        SELECT url, domain, koryciarski_llm_score, koryciarski_llm_reason, extracted_facts
        FROM analyzed WHERE koryciarski_llm_score = ? LIMIT 2
    """, [score]).df()
    for _, r in rows.iterrows():
        print(f'  [{r["domain"]}] https://{r["url"]}')
        print(f'  reason: {r["koryciarski_llm_reason"]}')
        for f in r['extracted_facts']:
            print(f'    {json.dumps({k: v for k, v in f.items() if k != "url"}, ensure_ascii=False)}')
        print()

=== Score 2 ===
  [rmf24.pl] https://rmf24.pl/fakty/polska/news-prokuratura-zaostrza-zarzuty-dla-szefa-fundacji-kidprotectpl,nId,1022474
  reason: Artykuł opisuje przypadki przywłaszczenia pieniędzy publicznych i wydatkowania ich na cele prywatne przez szefa fundacji, co może wskazywać na nadużycia finansowe. Jednak nie ma bezpośredniego opisu koryciarstwa w formie nepotyzmu, obsadzania stanowisk po znajomości czy konfliktów interesów. Opisane zachowania są bardziej związane z oszustwem finansowym niż koryciarstwem w sensie politycznym.
    {"justification": "Szef Fundacji Kidprotect.pl Jakub Ś.", "justification_in_text": "Szef Fundacji Kidprotect.pl Jakub Ś.", "person": "Jakub Ś.", "organization": "Fundacja Kidprotect.pl", "role": "szef", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}

  [wpolityce.pl] https://wpolityce.pl/polityka/508727-prezydent-w-debicy-ochrona-wartosci-filarem-rozwoju
  reason: Artykuł opisuje przemówienie Andrzeja Dudy w kontekście kampanii wyborczej, w którym prezydent krytykuje poprzednią kadencję, mówiąc o 'zwijaniu Polski' i polityce, która 'służyła nie wiadomo komu'. Może to sugerować krytykę nadużyć, ale nie zawiera konkretnych wskazówek na nepotyzm, konflikty interesów, obsadzanie stanowisk po znajomości czy korupcję. Brak bezpośrednich dowodów na koryciarstwo.
    {"justification": "Prezydent został przywitany...", "justification_in_text": "Prezydent został przywitany", "person": "Andrzej Duda", "organization": "Rzeczpospolita Polska", "role": "Prezydent", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}


=== Score 3 ===
  [wpolityce.pl] https://wpolityce.pl/polityka/717122-prok-ostrowski-oczekiwania-premiera-sa-ponad-prawem
  reason: Artykuł odnosi się do sytuacji w Prokuratorze Krajowej, wskazując na potencjalne ingerencje polityczne w działania prokuratury, co może sugerować konflikt interesów. Jednak nie ma bezpośredniego opisu nadużyć, nepotyzmu czy konkretnych przypadków koryciarstwa. Treść sugeruje, że Prokurator Generalny może ingerować w strukturę prokuratury, ale nie potwierdza to koryciarstwa w sposób jednoznaczny.
    {"justification": "Prok. Michał Ostrowski, zastępca Prokuratora Generalnego Adama Bodnara", "justification_in_text": "Prok. Michał Ostrowski, zastępca Prokuratora Generalnego Adama Bodnara", "person": "Michał Ostrowski", "organization": "Prokurator Generalny", "role": "zastępca Prokuratora Generalnego", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}

  [rmf24.pl] https://rmf24.pl/fakty/ekonomia/news-skandal-w-pkp-wydaly-27-milionow-choc-mogly-550-tysiecy,nId,965283
  reason: Artykuł opisuje nadużycia finansowe w grupie PKP, w tym wydatek 27 milionów złotych zamiast 550 tysięcy na system bezpieczeństwa informacji. Sprawa została zgłoszona do CBA, co sugeruje możliwe konflikty interesów lub inne nadużycia. Jednak nie ma bezpośredniego opisu nepotyzmu, obsadzania stanowisk po znajomości czy konkretnych przypadków koryciarstwa.
    {"justification": "Prezes grupy PKP Jakub Karnowski zaznacza...", "justification_in_text": "Prezes grupy PKP Jakub Karnowski zaznacza", "person": "Jakub Karnowski", "organization": "grupa PKP", "role": "prezes", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}

  [rmf24.pl] https://rmf24.pl/fakty/news-szef-cba-uslyszy-zarzuty-chodzi-min-o-lapowki,nId,132267
  reason: Artykuł opisuje konkretny przypadek podejrzenia korupcji i nadużyć przez szefa CBA, w tym wręczenie łapówek, fałszowanie dokumentów oraz przekroczenie uprawnień. Zawiera zarzuty sprawstwa kierowniczego, co wskazuje na koryciarstwo.

  reason: Artykuł opisuje konkretny przypadek korupcji w polskim futbolu, w tym zarzuty udziału w zorganizowanej grupie przestępczej, co wskazuje na nadużycia i koryciarstwo. Mimo że nie opisuje bezpośrednio nepotyzmu czy obsadzania stanowisk po znajomości, zawiera informacje o korupcji w kontekście publicznego stanowiska.

  [gospodarkapodkarpacka.pl] https://gospodarkapodkarpacka.pl/news/view/70707/zarzuty-i-areszt-za-zakupy-wegla-dla-rzadowej-agencji-rezerw-strategicznych-w-latach-2022-2023
  reason: Artykuł opisuje konkretną sprawę korupcyjną, w której funkcjonariusze Rządowej Agencji Rezerw Strategicznych wpłynęli na postępowania zakupowe, by zapewnić kontrakt spółce niekompetentnej, co doprowadziło do szkody publicznej. Opisane są nadużycia uprawnień, konflikt interesów i czerpanie korzyści majątkowych. Sprawa dotyczy bezpośrednio koryciarstwa w formie korupcji i nadużyć w administracji publicznej.
    {"justification": "byłego współpracownika Rządowej Agencji Rezerw Strategicznych", "justification_in_text": "byłego współpracownika Rządowej Agencji Rezerw Strategicznych", "person": "byłego współpracownika", "organization": "Rządowa Agencja Rezerw Strategicznych", "role": "współpracownik", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}

  [warszawa.onet.pl] https://warszawa.onet.pl/warszawa-cba-w-urzedzie-wojewodzkim-chodzi-o-budowe-hotelu-w-tle-sprawa-bylego/037tdxw
  reason: Artykuł opisuje konkretną sprawę korupcyjną, w której byłego burmistrza i przedsiębiorcę podejrzewa się o przyjmowanie i dawanie łapówek w zamian za korzystne decyzje administracyjne. Opisane są konkretna kwota łapówki (200 tys. zł), kontekst (warunki zabudowy), a także działania śledztwa (CBA, zabezpieczanie dokumentów). Jest to wyraźne potwierdzenie koryciarstwa w formie korupcji i nadużyć władzy.
    {"justification": "Artura W., ówczesnego burmistrza dzielnicy Włochy", "justification_in_text": "Artura W., ówczesnego burmistrza dzielnicy Włochy", "person": "Artur W.", "organization": "Miasto Warszawa", "role": "burmistrz dzielnicy Włochy", "party": null, "subject": null, "object": null, "relation": null}
    {"justification": "garażu należącym do rodziny", "justification_in_text": "garażu należącym do rodziny", "person": null, "organization": null, "role": null, "party": null, "subject": "Artur W.", "object": "rodzina", "relation": "rodzinny posiadłość garażu"}
In [ ]:

URLs per Polish Voivodeship (heatmap)¶

In [98]:
import ast, urllib.request, ssl, json
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

seed = pd.read_csv('../../../files/seed.csv')
seed['region'] = seed['Miasto'].apply(
    lambda x: ast.literal_eval(x)[0].split('_')[0]
    if pd.notna(x) and str(x).strip().startswith('[') else None
)
domain_region = seed[['Domena', 'region']].dropna()

url_counts = con.execute("""
    SELECT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain,
           count(*) AS urls
    FROM done_urls GROUP BY 1
""").df()

merged = url_counts.merge(domain_region, left_on='domain', right_on='Domena', how='inner')
region_urls = merged.groupby('region')['urls'].sum().reset_index()

# fetch NUTS2 GeoJSON
ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
nuts_url = 'https://gisco-services.ec.europa.eu/distribution/v2/nuts/geojson/NUTS_RG_20M_2021_4326_LEVL_2.geojson'
with urllib.request.urlopen(nuts_url, timeout=15, context=ctx) as r:
    nuts = json.loads(r.read().decode('utf-8'))
pl_features = [f for f in nuts['features'] if f['properties']['CNTR_CODE'] == 'PL']
pl_geojson = {'type': 'FeatureCollection', 'features': pl_features}

# map NUTS_NAME -> seed region name (Mazowieckie is two NUTS2 features)
nuts_name_map = {
    'Warszawski stołeczny': 'Mazowieckie',
    'Mazowiecki regionalny': 'Mazowieckie',
    'Łódzkie': 'Łódzkie', 'Świętokrzyskie': 'Świętokrzyskie',
    'Lubelskie': 'Lubelskie', 'Podkarpackie': 'Podkarpackie',
    'Podlaskie': 'Podlaskie', 'Małopolskie': 'Małopolskie',
    'Śląskie': 'Śląskie', 'Wielkopolskie': 'Wielkopolskie',
    'Zachodniopomorskie': 'Zachodniopomorskie', 'Lubuskie': 'Lubuskie',
    'Dolnośląskie': 'Dolnośląskie', 'Opolskie': 'Opolskie',
    'Kujawsko-pomorskie': 'Kujawsko-pomorskie',
    'Warmińsko-mazurskie': 'Warmińsko-mazurskie', 'Pomorskie': 'Pomorskie',
}
# build plot data keyed by NUTS_NAME, duplicating Mazowieckie count
url_by_region = dict(zip(region_urls['region'], region_urls['urls']))
plot_data = pd.DataFrame([
    {'nuts_name': nuts_name, 'urls': url_by_region.get(region, 0)}
    for nuts_name, region in nuts_name_map.items()
])

fig = px.choropleth(
    plot_data,
    geojson=pl_geojson,
    locations='nuts_name',
    featureidkey='properties.NUTS_NAME',
    color='urls',
    color_continuous_scale='YlOrRd',
    title='URLs per voivodeship',
    labels={'urls': 'URL count'},
)
fig.update_geos(fitbounds='locations', visible=False)
fig.update_layout(margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=40, b=0), height=500)
fig.show()
region_urls.sort_values('urls', ascending=False)
Out[98]:

URLs per Powiat (heatmap)¶

In [97]:
import ast, urllib.request, ssl, json
import pandas as pd
import plotly.express as px

# load seed.csv: domain -> list of powiaty
seed = pd.read_csv('../../../files/seed.csv')
def get_powiaty(x):
    if not pd.notna(x) or not str(x).strip().startswith('['): return []
    return [i.split('_')[1] for i in ast.literal_eval(x) if len(i.split('_')) >= 2]

rows = []
for _, r in seed.iterrows():
    for powiat in get_powiaty(r['Miasto']):
        rows.append({'domain': r['Domena'], 'powiat': powiat})
domain_powiat = pd.DataFrame(rows)

# count URLs per domain, join with powiat
url_counts = con.execute("""
    SELECT split_part(regexp_replace(url, '^https?://', ''), '/', 1) AS domain,
           count(*) AS urls
    FROM done_urls GROUP BY 1
""").df()

merged = url_counts.merge(domain_powiat, on='domain', how='inner')
powiat_urls = merged.groupby('powiat')['urls'].sum().reset_index()

# fetch powiaty GeoJSON
ctx = ssl.create_default_context()
geo_url = 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/ppatrzyk/polska-geojson/master/powiaty/powiaty-medium.geojson'
with urllib.request.urlopen(geo_url, timeout=15, context=ctx) as r:
    geojson = json.loads(r.read())

# GeoJSON names have 'powiat ' prefix, strip it to match seed.csv
for f in geojson['features']:
    f['properties']['powiat'] = f['properties']['nazwa'].replace('powiat ', '', 1)

fig = px.choropleth(
    powiat_urls,
    geojson=geojson,
    locations='powiat',
    featureidkey='properties.powiat',
    color='urls',
    color_continuous_scale='YlOrRd',
    title='URLs per powiat',
    labels={'urls': 'URL count'},
)
fig.update_geos(fitbounds='locations', visible=False)
fig.update_layout(margin=dict(l=0, r=0, t=40, b=0), height=550)
fig.show()
powiat_urls.sort_values('urls', ascending=False).head(20)
Out[97]:

Article Publication Timeline (from ld_json datePublished)¶

In [100]:
import json as _json
from collections import Counter
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.ticker as mticker

parsed_path = '../../../versioned/article_parsed/article_parsed.jsonl'
year_counts = Counter()
month_counts = Counter()

with open(parsed_path) as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if not line: continue
        try:
            r = _json.loads(line)
            if r.get('parse_status') != 'ok': continue
            lj = r.get('ld_json')
            if not isinstance(lj, dict): continue
            date = lj.get('datePublished') or lj.get('dateCreated')
            if not date:
                for item in (lj.get('@graph') or []):
                    if isinstance(item, dict):
                        date = item.get('datePublished') or item.get('dateCreated')
                        if date: break
            if not date or not str(date)[:4].isdigit(): continue
            d = str(date)[:10]
            year = int(d[:4])
            if not (2000 <= year <= 2026): continue
            year_counts[year] += 1
            if len(d) >= 7:
                month_counts[d[:7]] += 1
        except: pass

print(f'Total dated articles: {sum(year_counts.values()):,}')

# --- year bar chart ---
fig, axes = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(14, 10))

years = sorted(year_counts)
ax = axes[0]
ax.bar(years, [year_counts[y] for y in years], color='steelblue')
ax.set_title('Articles per year (datePublished from ld_json)')
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Articles')
ax.xaxis.set_major_locator(mticker.MultipleLocator(1))
ax.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{int(x):,}'))

# --- month bar chart (2019+) ---
months = sorted(k for k in month_counts if k >= '2019')
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.bar(range(len(months)), [month_counts[m] for m in months], color='steelblue')
ax2.set_title('Articles per month (2019+)')
ax2.set_ylabel('Articles')
# label every 12 months
tick_pos = [i for i, m in enumerate(months) if m.endswith('-01')]
ax2.set_xticks(tick_pos)
ax2.set_xticklabels([months[i][:4] for i in tick_pos], rotation=45)
ax2.yaxis.set_major_formatter(mticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f'{int(x):,}'))

# --- Polish elections as vertical lines ---
wybory = {
    '2019-05': 'EU parl.',
    '2019-10': 'Sejm/Senat',
    '2020-06': 'Prezydent R1',
    '2020-07': 'Prezydent R2',
    '2023-10': 'Sejm/Senat',
    '2024-04': 'Samorządowe',
    '2024-06': 'EU parl.',
    '2025-05': 'Prezydent R1',
    '2025-06': 'Prezydent R2',
}
for month_str, label in wybory.items():
    if month_str in months:
        xi = months.index(month_str)
        ax2.axvline(xi, color='red', alpha=0.7, linewidth=1.2, linestyle='--')
        ax2.text(xi + 0.3, ax2.get_ylim()[1] * 0.92, label,
                 fontsize=7, color='red', rotation=90, va='top')

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

Total dated articles: 335,017

Małe strony, które umiemy parsować i mają wysoki score¶

In [108]:
df = con.execute("""
    WITH score_stats AS (
        SELECT
            split_part(url, '/', 1) AS domain,
            count(*) AS scored,
            sum(CASE WHEN koryciarski_llm_score >= 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS score_3plus,
            sum(CASE WHEN koryciarski_llm_score >= 4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS score_4plus,
            sum(CASE WHEN koryciarski_llm_score = 5 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS score_5,
            round(avg(koryciarski_llm_score), 2) AS avg_score
        FROM scores
        WHERE error IS NULL AND llm_is_article = true
        GROUP BY 1
    ),
    parse_stats AS (
        SELECT
            split_part(url, '/', 1) AS domain,
            count(*) AS total_parsed,
            sum(CASE WHEN parse_status = 'ok' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS ok_pages
        FROM parsed
        GROUP BY 1
    ),
    best_url AS (
        SELECT split_part(url, '/', 1) AS domain,
               'https://' || arg_max(url, koryciarski_llm_score) AS top_url,
               max(koryciarski_llm_score) AS top_score
        FROM scores
        WHERE error IS NULL AND llm_is_article = true
        GROUP BY 1
    )
    SELECT
        s.domain, p.total_parsed, p.ok_pages,
        round(100.0 * p.ok_pages / NULLIF(p.total_parsed, 0), 1) AS ok_pct,
        s.scored, s.score_3plus,
        round(100.0 * s.score_3plus / NULLIF(s.scored, 0), 1) AS pct_3plus,
        s.score_4plus, s.score_5, s.avg_score,
        b.top_url, b.top_score
    FROM score_stats s
    JOIN parse_stats p ON s.domain = p.domain
    JOIN best_url b ON s.domain = b.domain
    WHERE s.scored BETWEEN 5 AND 500
      AND s.score_3plus >= 2
      AND round(100.0 * s.score_3plus / NULLIF(s.scored, 0), 1) >= 20
      AND round(100.0 * p.ok_pages / NULLIF(p.total_parsed, 0), 1) >= 50
    ORDER BY pct_3plus DESC, score_5 DESC
    LIMIT 40
""").df()

print('Top scoring URL per domain:')
for _, r in df.iterrows():
    print(f"  [{int(r['top_score'])}] {r['domain']:<35} {r['top_url']}")

df.drop(columns=['top_url', 'top_score'])

Top scoring URL per domain:

  [4] wiadomosci.wp.pl                    https://wiadomosci.wp.pl/tak-konfederacja-dzieli-pieniadze-z-przyszlej-subwencji-ujawniamy-tresc-tajnego-spotkania-partii-korwin-mikkego-6432947540502145a
  [5] jawnylublin.pl                      https://jawnylublin.pl/jak-agent-krzysiek-i-agent-rafal-sprawy-zalatwiali-funkcjonariusze-cba-z-lublina-przed-sadem
  [4] portalwarszawski.com.pl             https://portalwarszawski.com.pl/2025/06/13/aktualnosci/nasz-news-a-auksel-sekutowicz-z-miejskiego-urzedu-zrobila-posredniak-pracy-dla-czlonkow-ko
  [4] miastozwizja.pl                     https://miastozwizja.pl/bronia-polskich-lasow-przed-kim
  [3] tvswietokrzyska.pl                  https://tvswietokrzyska.pl/artykul/6059,wydanie-z-dnia-10-06-2014
  [4] krowoderska.pl                      https://krowoderska.pl/jerzy-wenderlich-krakow-azyl-dla-przegranych-politykow
  [5] kurierzawiercianski.pl              https://kurierzawiercianski.pl/artykul/8769,lekarka-przewodniczaca-rady-powiatu-z-zarzutami-grozi-jej-do-8-lat-wiezienia
  [3] infobusko.pl                        https://infobusko.pl/%f0%9f%9f%a5-lukasz-nowak-czy-dla-jerzego-czysciciela-szydlowskiego-mieszkancy-sa-smieciami-bulwersujaca-wypowiedz-burmistrza-na-xix-radzie-miejskiej/comment-page-2

  [5] gazetamyszkowska.pl                 https://gazetamyszkowska.pl/artykul/12823,lekarze-wojciech-p-i-beata-j-b-razem-7-oskarzonych-akt-oskarzenia-w-sadzie
